exercises.title: and, or, not Truth Tables
exercises.description: Print the truth table results for and, or, and not with the given boolean values.
exercises.starterCode:
a = True
b = False

# TODO: Print results of logical operations
print(a and b)    # and: both must be True
print(a or b)     # or: at least one must be True
print(not a)      # not: reverses the boolean
print(not b)      # not: reverses the boolean
print(a and a)    # True and True
print(b or b)     # False or False
exercises.expectedOutput:
False
True
False
True
True
False
hints: and: both sides must be True for the result to be True.
or: at least one side must be True for the result to be True.
not: flips True to False and False to True.

exercises.title: Combining Conditions
exercises.description: Use logical operators to combine multiple conditions in a single if statement.
exercises.starterCode:
age = 25
has_license = True
has_insurance = True

# TODO: Can drive only if age >= 16 AND has_license AND has_insurance
if age >= 16 and has_license and has_insurance:
    print("You can drive.")
else:
    print("You cannot drive.")

income = 45000
credit_score = 720

# TODO: Eligible for loan if income >= 50000 OR credit_score >= 700
if income >= 50000 or credit_score >= 700:
    print("Eligible for loan.")
else:
    print("Not eligible for loan.")
exercises.expectedOutput:
You can drive.
Eligible for loan.
hints: All three conditions must be True for 'can drive'.
Only one of the two conditions needs to be True for 'eligible for loan'.

exercises.title: Short-Circuit Evaluation
exercises.description: Python stops evaluating 'and' as soon as it finds a False, and stops 'or' as soon as it finds a True. Trace through these expressions and print their results.
exercises.starterCode:
x = 10

# Short-circuit with 'and': stops at first False
print(x > 5 and x < 20)     # True and True
print(x > 5 and x > 20)     # True and False
print(x > 20 and x < 30)    # False — second part is not evaluated

# Short-circuit with 'or': stops at first True
print(x > 5 or x > 100)     # True — second part is not evaluated
print(x > 20 or x < 5)      # False or False
print(x < 0 or x > 5)       # False or True
exercises.expectedOutput:
True
False
False
True
False
True
hints: and stops early at the first False; the whole expression is False.
or stops early at the first True; the whole expression is True.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Logical Operators
warmup.description: and, or, not combine boolean expressions. What does this print?
warmup.starterCode:
is_weekend = True
is_sunny = False

print(is_weekend and is_sunny)  # both must be True
print(is_weekend or is_sunny)   # at least one must be True
print(not is_sunny)             # reverse
